passage_title: Britain's canals - the solution to overcrowded roads?

sentences.sentence: It's hard to imagine that only a decade or so ago many of the nation's canals were little more than the last resting place for abandoned shopping trolleys.
sentences.translation: 很难想象，就在大约十年前，英国许多运河还不过是废弃购物车最后的归宿。

structure.text: It's hard to imagine
that only a decade or so ago many of the nation's canals were little more than the last resting place for abandoned shopping trolleys

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，to imagine 承载真正内容，that 从句是 imagine 的宾语。
little more than 表示“只不过是”，带有贬低意味。

sentences.sentence: There's still work to be done, but their transformation has been remarkable.
sentences.translation: 虽然仍有工作要做，但这些运河的转变已经十分显著。

structure.text: There's still work to be done
but their transformation has been remarkable

difficult_points: to be done 是被动不定式，后置修饰 work。
their 指上一句的 canals；现在完成时强调转变迄今的成果。

sentences.sentence: Projects such as Castlefield in Manchester and Brindley Place in Birmingham have transformed city-centre canals from stagnant reminders of a fading industrial past to the epitome of urban cool.
sentences.translation: 曼彻斯特的卡斯尔菲尔德和伯明翰的布林德利广场等项目，已把市中心运河从衰落工业时代的停滞遗迹，改造成都市时尚的典范。

structure.text: Projects such as Castlefield in Manchester and Brindley Place in Birmingham
have transformed city-centre canals
from stagnant reminders of a fading industrial past to the epitome of urban cool

difficult_points: such as 引出 projects 的两个实例。
transform A from X to Y 表示把A从一种状态转变为另一种状态。

sentences.sentence: However, 21st-century priorities dictate that the rehabilitation of this 18th-century motorway system cannot stop there.
sentences.translation: 然而，21世纪的优先需求决定了：对这一18世纪“高速路系统”的复兴不能止步于此。

structure.text: 21st-century priorities
dictate
that the rehabilitation of this 18th-century motorway system cannot stop there

difficult_points: dictate that 表示客观形势要求某事，而非口头听写。
this 18th-century motorway system 是对运河网的比喻性称呼；there 指前述景观改造。

sentences.sentence: Canals and navigable rivers form a major transport network, in need of only piecemeal investment, and with the spare capacity to take away the need for hundreds of thousands of lorry journeys.
sentences.translation: 运河和可通航河流构成重要运输网，只需零散投资，而且还有富余运力，可省去数十万趟卡车运输。

structure.text: Canals and navigable rivers form a major transport network
in need of only piecemeal investment
with the spare capacity to take away the need for hundreds of thousands of lorry journeys

difficult_points: 逗号后两个成分都补充说明 transport network。
to take away ... 修饰 capacity，说明富余运力用途。

sentences.sentence: In the second half of the 18th century, canals drove the industrial revolution.
sentences.translation: 18世纪下半叶，运河推动了工业革命。

structure.text: In the second half of the 18th century
canals drove the industrial revolution

difficult_points: drove 是 drive 的过去式，此处意为推动。
时间状语界定运河发挥历史作用的时期。

sentences.sentence: Today, authorities want them to drive congestion off the roads.
sentences.translation: 如今，当局希望运河能把拥堵赶出公路。

structure.text: authorities
want them to drive congestion off the roads

difficult_points: them 指 canals，want + 宾语 + 不定式表示希望其做某事。
drive 延续上一句修辞，此处意为缓解道路拥堵。

sentences.sentence: Last month, for example, the European Commission proposed a seven-year plan to shift large amounts of freight from roads to inland waterways.
sentences.translation: 例如，上个月欧盟委员会提出一项七年计划，把大量货运从公路转移到内陆水道。

structure.text: the European Commission proposed a seven-year plan
to shift large amounts of freight from roads to inland waterways

difficult_points: to shift 后置修饰 plan，说明计划内容。
shift A from X to Y 表示改变运输渠道。

sentences.sentence: Europe's enthusiasm comes as no surprise.
sentences.translation: 欧洲对此的热情并不令人意外。

structure.text: Europe's enthusiasm
comes as no surprise

difficult_points: Europe 是转喻，指欧洲各国及机构。
come as no surprise 表示完全在意料之中。

sentences.sentence: Freight traffic is expected to grow by a third in the next decade.
sentences.translation: 预计未来十年货运量将增长三分之一。

structure.text: Freight traffic
is expected to grow by a third
in the next decade

difficult_points: is expected to 是被动报道结构。
by a third 表示增长幅度，而不是增长到三分之一。

sentences.sentence: The cost of pollution and congestion is set to swallow one per cent of Europe's entire GDP by 2010.
sentences.translation: 到2010年，污染和拥堵的代价预计将吞噬欧洲GDP总量的1%。

structure.text: The cost of pollution and congestion
is set to swallow one per cent of Europe's entire GDP

difficult_points: be set to 表示预计即将发生。
swallow 是比喻，表示消耗大量经济资源。

sentences.sentence: "With a fleet of 11000 vessels and a capacity equalling 10000 trains or 440000 trucks, inland waterways can make transport in Europe more efficient, reliable and environmental friendly," says Jacques Barrot, vice-president of the European Commission in charge of transport.
sentences.translation: “内陆水道拥有1.1万艘船，其运力相当于1万列火车或44万辆卡车，因此能使欧洲运输更加高效、可靠且环保，”欧盟委员会负责交通事务的副主席雅克·巴罗说。

structure.text: With a fleet of 11000 vessels and a capacity equalling 10000 trains or 440000 trucks
inland waterways can make transport in Europe more efficient, reliable and environmental friendly
says Jacques Barrot

difficult_points: equalling 是现在分词，后置修饰 capacity。
make + 宾语 + 形容词构成宾补；environmental friendly 是源文非常规表达。

sentences.sentence: "Europe cannot afford to leave that potential untapped."
sentences.translation: “欧洲不能任由这一潜力得不到开发。”

structure.text: Europe
cannot afford to leave that potential untapped

difficult_points: cannot afford to 表示无法承受做某事的后果。
leave + 宾语 + 过去分词 untapped 表示让潜力保持未开发状态。

sentences.sentence: Mainland Europe has never, in fairness, left it completely untapped.
sentences.translation: 公平地说，欧洲大陆从未让这一潜力完全闲置。

structure.text: Mainland Europe
has never, in fairness, left it completely untapped

difficult_points: in fairness 是插入性评注，表示公允地说。
it 指水运潜力，leave it untapped 是宾补结构。

sentences.sentence: The canals of the low countries and the rivers of central and eastern Europe buzzed with the sound of freight barges long after British industry had thrown in its lot with railways and roads.
sentences.translation: 在英国工业早已选择依靠铁路和公路之后很久，低地国家的运河以及中东欧的河流仍回响着货运驳船声。

structure.text: The canals of the low countries and the rivers of central and eastern Europe buzzed with the sound of freight barges
long after British industry had thrown in its lot with railways and roads

difficult_points: long after 引导时间从句，过去完成时表示英国选择在先。
throw in one's lot with 表示决定与某方共同发展。

sentences.sentence: Attempts to revive freight on British canals have been hampered by the fact that their heyday lasted barely 60 years, and they were first considered obsolete 150 years ago.
sentences.translation: 复兴英国运河货运的尝试一直受阻，因为其鼎盛期仅持续约60年，而且早在150年前就首次被视为过时。

structure.text: Attempts to revive freight on British canals have been hampered
by the fact that their heyday lasted barely 60 years, and they were first considered obsolete 150 years ago

difficult_points: to revive ... 修饰 Attempts；have been hampered 是现在完成时被动。
that 从句解释 fact，their 与 they 均指 British canals。

sentences.sentence: For much of the intervening period, many have simply been left to rot.
sentences.translation: 在其间的大部分时期，许多运河只是被任由荒废腐朽。

structure.text: For much of the intervening period
many have simply been left to rot

difficult_points: many 省略 canals，由上文补足。
leave + 宾语 + to do 的被动形式表示任其腐朽。

sentences.sentence: "Our network was in decline for a long time compared to much of Europe," says Eugene Baston of British Waterways.
sentences.translation: 英国水道局的尤金·巴斯顿说：“与欧洲许多地区相比，我们的水运网络长期处于衰退。”

structure.text: Our network was in decline for a long time compared to much of Europe
says Eugene Baston of British Waterways

difficult_points: compared to 是过去分词比较短语。
引语后的倒装式 reporting clause 主语是 Eugene Baston。

sentences.sentence: "Whereas other countries developed road and rail transport but carried on using their waterways as well, our canals were neglected.
sentences.translation: “其他国家在发展公路和铁路运输的同时仍继续使用水道，而我们的运河却遭到忽视。

structure.text: Whereas other countries developed road and rail transport but carried on using their waterways as well
our canals were neglected

difficult_points: Whereas 引导对比从句，内部 developed 与 carried on using 并列。
该引语在源文句末缺少结束引号，按原样保留。

sentences.sentence: In fact many European countries actually enlarged their canals 100 years ago."
sentences.translation: 事实上，许多欧洲国家早在100年前就扩建了运河。”

structure.text: many European countries
actually enlarged their canals 100 years ago

difficult_points: In fact 与 actually 共同加强事实语气。
句末双引号承接上一句未闭合的引语。

sentences.sentence: That decline in Britain has been reversed, first by leisure seekers and more recently by industry.
sentences.translation: 英国运河的衰退已被扭转，最初靠休闲活动参与者，近来则靠工业。

structure.text: That decline in Britain has been reversed
first by leisure seekers and more recently by industry

difficult_points: That decline 指上一段所述运河衰落。
两个 by 短语并列，first 与 more recently 标出先后阶段。

sentences.sentence: Boaters, anglers, walkers and cyclists now benefit from around 4000 miles of navigable waterways and the paths and trails that run alongside them.
sentences.translation: 如今，划船者、垂钓者、步行者和骑行者都受益于约4000英里的可通航水道及沿岸小径。

structure.text: Boaters, anglers, walkers and cyclists
now benefit from around 4000 miles of navigable waterways and the paths and trails
that run alongside them

difficult_points: that 修饰 paths and trails；them 指 waterways。
benefit from 后并列水道与沿岸路径两类资源。

sentences.sentence: Waterside living is fashionable, and city-centre canals have been a focus for urban renewal, And, despite our obsession with road transport, environmental considerations are forcing government and business to mm the clock back 200 years and--at least in a minor way--get our waterways working again.
sentences.translation: 滨水居住已成为时尚，市中心运河也成为城市更新的重点；尽管我们迷恋公路运输，环境因素正迫使政府和企业把时钟拨回200年前，并至少在较小程度上让水道重新运转。

structure.text: Waterside living is fashionable
and city-centre canals have been a focus for urban renewal
environmental considerations are forcing government and business to mm the clock back 200 years and--at least in a minor way--get our waterways working again

difficult_points: despite 后接名词短语表示让步。
force + 宾语 + to do，后面 turn/get 两动作并列；源文 mm 应为 turn 的扫描错误，原样保留。
句中大写 And 和双连字符均为源文异常。

sentences.sentence: In fact, industrial goods such as coal, steel, aggregates and petroleum have never completely disappeared from large rivers and designated commercial waterways.
sentences.translation: 事实上，煤、钢材、骨料和石油等工业货物从未完全退出大河和指定商业水道。

structure.text: industrial goods such as coal, steel, aggregates and petroleum
have never completely disappeared from large rivers and designated commercial waterways

difficult_points: such as 引出 industrial goods 的例子。
现在完成时强调从过去到现在从未完全消失。

sentences.sentence: Barges on the river Severn have recently started carrying the equivalent of 34000 lorry loads of aggregates each year, the first freight traffic on the river for a decade.
sentences.translation: 塞文河上的驳船最近开始每年运输相当于3.4万卡车载量的骨料，这是该河十年来首次恢复货运。

structure.text: Barges on the river Severn have recently started carrying the equivalent of 34000 lorry loads of aggregates each year
the first freight traffic on the river for a decade

difficult_points: the equivalent of 表示以卡车载量折算。
句末名词短语是对整个事件的同位补充。

sentences.sentence: British Waterways, which owns about half of the country's navigable inland waterways, carded the equivalent of 64000 25-ton lorry loads of freight in 2004.
sentences.translation: 英国水道局拥有全国约一半的内陆可通航水道，2004年运输了相当于6.4万车、每车25吨的货物。

structure.text: British Waterways
which owns about half of the country's navigable inland waterways
carded the equivalent of 64000 25-ton lorry loads of freight in 2004

difficult_points: which 是非限制性定语从句，补充机构所拥有的水道。
carded 是源文疑似 carried 的扫描错误，按原文保留。

sentences.sentence: The organisation says these figures are certain to increase as new schemes start, and environmentalists hope they will.
sentences.translation: 该机构表示，随着新项目启动，这些数字必然上升，环保人士也希望如此。

structure.text: The organisation says these figures are certain to increase as new schemes start
and environmentalists hope they will

difficult_points: says 后省略 that；as 引导时间从句。
they will 省略 increase，they 指 figures。

sentences.sentence: Carrying freight by water uses about a quarter of the energy of an equivalent road journey.
sentences.translation: 水运货物的能耗约为同等公路运输的四分之一。

structure.text: Carrying freight by water
uses about a quarter of the energy of an equivalent road journey

difficult_points: Carrying 是动名词作主语。
a quarter of 比较的是两种运输方式的能耗。

sentences.sentence: In comparison to lorries, barges produce low emissions, low noise and are visually unobtrusive.
sentences.translation: 与卡车相比，驳船排放低、噪声小，视觉上也不显眼。

structure.text: In comparison to lorries
barges produce low emissions, low noise and are visually unobtrusive

difficult_points: produce 与 are 是共享主语的并列谓语。
visually unobtrusive 表示不会明显破坏景观。

sentences.sentence: "We think that anything that can take freight off the roads needs to be fully explored," says Tony Bosworth, transport campaigner for Friends of the Earth.
sentences.translation: 地球之友交通倡议者托尼·博斯沃思说：“我们认为，任何能把货运从公路转走的办法都需要充分探索。”

structure.text: We think that anything that can take freight off the roads needs to be fully explored
that can take freight off the roads
says Tony Bosworth

difficult_points: 第一个 that 引导宾语从句，第二个限定 anything。
needs to be explored 是被动结构。

sentences.sentence: "Canals can do that.
sentences.translation: “运河能够做到这一点。

structure.text: Canals can do that

difficult_points: that 指把货运从公路转移出去。
源文该句开引号但未在句末闭合。

sentences.sentence: They can help cut the pollution that causes climate change."
sentences.translation: 它们有助于减少导致气候变化的污染。”

structure.text: They can help cut the pollution
that causes climate change

difficult_points: They 指 canals；help 后接不带 to 的动词原形。
that 修饰 pollution，句末引号闭合上一句引语。

sentences.sentence: There is a limit to what canals can carry.
sentences.translation: 运河所能运输的货物存在限度。

structure.text: There is a limit
to what canals can carry

difficult_points: what 从句作介词 to 的宾语。
what 融合先行词，意为运河能运的东西。

sentences.sentence: The slow pace of water travel does not fit well with the limited shelf-life of fresh produce.
sentences.translation: 水运速度缓慢，与生鲜农产品有限的保质期并不相配。

structure.text: The slow pace of water travel
does not fit well with the limited shelf-life of fresh produce

difficult_points: fit with 表示与条件相适应。
shelf-life 指产品可安全销售或食用的期限。

sentences.sentence: If supermarkets won't trust their cherry tomatoes to water, they might trust the waste paper and plastic that protects them.
sentences.translation: 即使超市不愿把小番茄交给水运，也可能愿意让保护番茄的废纸和塑料走水路。

structure.text: If supermarkets won't trust their cherry tomatoes to water
they might trust the waste paper and plastic that protects them

difficult_points: trust A to B 表示把A交由B处理。
that 修饰 waste paper and plastic；them 指 tomatoes。

sentences.sentence: Many of the proposals to utilise Britain's waterways are based around waste management and recycling schemes.
sentences.translation: 许多利用英国水道的提案都围绕废物管理和回收计划展开。

structure.text: Many of the proposals to utilise Britain's waterways
are based around waste management and recycling schemes

difficult_points: to utilise 后置修饰 proposals。
be based around 表示以某主题为核心。

sentences.sentence: For example, a pilot scheme in Hackney, east London, has seen municipal waste collected by truck and transferred to barge for transportation to a reprocessing plant.
sentences.translation: 例如，伦敦东部哈克尼的一项试点计划把城市垃圾先由卡车收集，再转装驳船运往再处理厂。

structure.text: a pilot scheme in Hackney, east London, has seen municipal waste collected by truck and transferred to barge
for transportation to a reprocessing plant

difficult_points: has seen 在此表示该计划见证或实现某结果。
collected 与 transferred 是并列过去分词宾补。

sentences.sentence: In the future, the scheme could remove 300000 dustcart miles from the borough's streets every year.
sentences.translation: 未来，该计划每年可减少该区街道上30万英里的垃圾车行程。

structure.text: the scheme
could remove 300000 dustcart miles from the borough's streets every year

difficult_points: dustcart miles 是以垃圾车行驶英里数衡量的交通量。
remove ... from ... 表示减少道路上的这些行程。

sentences.sentence: Current arrangements could be just the tip of the iceberg.
sentences.translation: 现有安排可能只是冰山一角。

structure.text: Current arrangements
could be just the tip of the iceberg

difficult_points: could 表示推测而非过去能力。
the tip of the iceberg 比喻已显现的部分只是很小一部分。

sentences.sentence: Planning permission has been given for a Powerday recycling plant at Willesden Junction, a site that sits on the intersection of road, rail and canal networks.
sentences.translation: 位于威尔斯登交汇处的Powerday回收厂已获规划许可，该地点处在公路、铁路和运河网络的交汇处。

structure.text: Planning permission has been given for a Powerday recycling plant at Willesden Junction
a site that sits on the intersection of road, rail and canal networks

difficult_points: has been given 是现在完成时被动。
a site 是 Willesden Junction 的同位语，that 从句修饰 site。

sentences.sentence: "The plant will have the capacity to handle a million and a half tons of waste every year, but the amount carried by road will be capped at 500000 tons," says Ed Fox of British Waterways London.
sentences.translation: 英国水道局伦敦分部的埃德·福克斯说：“该厂每年可处理150万吨废物，但公路运输量将被限制在50万吨。”

structure.text: The plant will have the capacity to handle a million and a half tons of waste every year
but the amount carried by road will be capped at 500000 tons

difficult_points: to handle 修饰 capacity；carried by road 修饰 amount。
be capped at 表示被设定上限。

sentences.sentence: "If they want to grow the business, they will have to work with us."
sentences.translation: “如果他们想扩大业务，就必须与我们合作。”

structure.text: If they want to grow the business
they will have to work with us

difficult_points: If 引导真实条件句，主句用 will have to。
they 指工厂运营方，us 指英国水道局。

sentences.sentence: Fox says getting freight back on the canals has been "a nice idea" for 50 years, but until recently little more than an idea.
sentences.translation: 福克斯说，让货运重回运河50年来一直是个“好想法”，但直到最近也仅仅是想法。

structure.text: Fox says getting freight back on the canals has been "a nice idea" for 50 years
but until recently little more than an idea

difficult_points: getting freight ... 是动名词短语作 says 后宾语从句的主语。
but 后省略 it has been；little more than 表示不过是。

sentences.sentence: "The Powerday project, on the other hand, is proof of what really can be done."
sentences.translation: “而Powerday项目证明了真正能够做到什么。”

structure.text: The Powerday project
is proof of what really can be done

difficult_points: on the other hand 是插入性对比语。
what 从句作 of 的宾语，what 在从句中作主语。

sentences.sentence: Though details have yet to be decided, British Waterways believes the most appropriate way to transport some of the building materials destined for London's giant Olympic construction project is by the network of waterways that links the Thames and east London.
sentences.translation: 尽管细节尚未决定，英国水道局认为，把部分运往伦敦大型奥运建设项目的建材通过连接泰晤士河与东伦敦的水道网络运输，是最合适的方式。

structure.text: Though details have yet to be decided
British Waterways believes
the most appropriate way to transport some of the building materials destined for London's giant Olympic construction project is by the network of waterways that links the Thames and east London

difficult_points: have yet to be decided 表示尚未决定。
destined for 是过去分词短语修饰 materials；that 从句修饰 waterways。

sentences.sentence: The Olympic Delivery Authority says: "It's being looked at and the final solution could well involve some transportation by water.
sentences.translation: 奥运交付管理局表示：“此事正在研究之中，最终方案很可能包含部分水运。

structure.text: The Olympic Delivery Authority says
It's being looked at
and the final solution could well involve some transportation by water

difficult_points: is being looked at 是进行时被动，表示正在审议。
could well 表示很可能；源文引语在句末未闭合。

sentences.sentence: What exactly we do will be based on a range of factors, but one of those will be sustainability."
sentences.translation: 我们具体怎么做将取决于多种因素，其中之一是可持续性。”

structure.text: What exactly we do
will be based on a range of factors
but one of those will be sustainability

difficult_points: What 从句作整句主语，what 在从句中作 do 的宾语。
those 指 factors；句末引号闭合上一句引语。

sentences.sentence: Their gentle pace will always make canals a niche player in a busy world, but after 200 years of neglect, the tide is starting to turn.
sentences.translation: 运河缓慢的速度注定使其在忙碌世界中只是小众角色，但经历200年的忽视后，局势正开始转变。

structure.text: Their gentle pace will always make canals a niche player in a busy world
but after 200 years of neglect, the tide is starting to turn

difficult_points: make canals a niche player 是“使役动词 + 宾语 + 宾补”。
the tide is starting to turn 是比喻，表示趋势开始逆转。

expressions.text: the tide is starting to turn
